title: Causes of shigellosis (Shigella) - Canada.ca
content:
## Causes of shigellosis ( Shigella )
Learn about the causes of shigellosis, how it's spread and where it's found.
- Cause of shigellosis
- How shigellosis is spread
- Where shigellosis is found

## Cause of shigellosis
Shigellosis is an illness of the intestines caused by bacteria called Shigella .

## How shigellosis is spread
Shigellosis is easily spread and you can become infected by:
- eating contaminated foods
- drinking contaminated liquids, including: drinking water other drinks recreational water such as rivers, lakes and other coastal waters
- recreational water such as rivers, lakes and other coastal waters
- touching contaminated surfaces, such as: diaper pails bathroom doorknobs
- exposure to the feces of an infected (or recently recovered) person during sexual activity, including direct or indirect oral-anal contact
- swimming and bathing in contaminated recreational waters, such as: lakes swimming pools
- touching your food or your mouth with hands that have become contaminated by: changing the diaper of an infected child caring for an infected person who may have the bacteria on their hands or body
- changing the diaper of an infected child
- caring for an infected person who may have the bacteria on their hands or body

### How food and water become contaminated
Food can become contaminated with Shigella when:
- it's handled by a person who: didn't properly wash their hands after touching: contaminated foods animals or animal environments, including food and treats is infected with Shigella and didn't properly wash their hands after using the bathroom
- didn't properly wash their hands after touching: contaminated foods animals or animal environments, including food and treats
- animals or animal environments, including food and treats
- is infected with Shigella and didn't properly wash their hands after using the bathroom
- it's prepared on a surface that came into contact with other foods or water contaminated with Shigella cross contamination can happen because of unsanitary food handling practices
- cross contamination can happen because of unsanitary food handling practices
- it comes into contact with contaminated manure or water when it's grown and harvested
- flies spread the bacteria from contaminated feces to uncovered foods
Foods that have been found to be contaminated with Shigella include:
- fruits and vegetables
- raw oysters and shellfish
- prepared salads, including: fruit tuna pasta potato lettuce chicken
Water, including untreated drinking water, can become contaminated with Shigella. This happens when feces from infected people or animals enter the water supply.

## Where shigellosis is found
Shigella is naturally found in the intestines of humans.
